---
title: Entra ID Dynamic Group Provisioning (OrgTree UIAO_152) — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: entra-dynamic-groups
adapter-class: modernization
mission-class: integration
status: active
phase: phase-1
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/modernization-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# Entra ID Dynamic Group Provisioning (OrgTree UIAO_152) — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `active` · **Class:** `modernization` · **Mission:** `integration` · **Phase:** `phase-1`

**Canon source:** `canon/modernization-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The Entra ID Dynamic Group Provisioning Adapter is the **OrgPath actuator** of the UIAO substrate — the component that turns canonical OrgPath values into policy-targetable Entra ID security groups. As a modernization-class integration adapter, it provisions one OrgTree-* dynamic security group per UIAO_152 library entry, with membership rules that reference the OrgPath extension attribute written by the [`active-directory`](../active-directory/active-directory.html) and [`entra-device-orgpath`](../entra-device-orgpath/entra-device-orgpath.html) adapters.

Three primary functions:

1. **Dynamic group provisioning.** Creates one Entra ID dynamic security group per UIAO_152 row, with the membership rule generated deterministically from the OrgPath prefix and operator (`-eq`, `-startsWith`). Groups follow the canonical naming convention `OrgTree-<NodeId>` so policy authors can refer to them by stable identifier rather than by display name.
2. **Membership rule maintenance.** Patches existing groups when the UIAO_152 library changes — for example, when an organizational segment's NodeId, scope operator, or evaluation precedence is updated. The adapter never silently rewrites a membership rule that has drifted away from canon; out-of-band edits surface as findings.
3. **Phantom group detection.** Identifies Entra groups whose name matches the OrgTree-* convention but whose UIAO_152 row has been retired or whose membership rule no longer matches canon. Phantom groups are **not** auto-deleted; per UIAO_152 §Drift they route through the Workflow 3 review in UIAO_155 so a human approves the deprovisioning.

These groups are the connective tissue for every downstream OrgPath consumer: [`entra-admin-units`](../entra-admin-units/entra-admin-units.html) scopes role assignments by them, [`entra-policy-targeting`](../entra-policy-targeting/entra-policy-targeting.html) targets Intune profiles to them, and Conditional Access and PIM bindings reference them by object id.

**Implementation:** `src/uiao/adapters/entra_dynamic_groups.py`. `plan()` and `apply(dry_run=True)` are offline-safe; `apply(dry_run=False)` issues `POST /groups` and `PATCH /groups/{id}` against Microsoft Graph.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `dynamic-membership-groups`, `orgpath-governed-groups`

**Reads:** UIAO_152 dynamic-group library (executable canon at `canon/data/orgpath/dynamic-groups.yaml`), UIAO_151 codebook for OrgPath prefix validation, and the live Entra ID group state via Microsoft Graph (filtered to the OrgTree-* naming convention).

**Emits:** `dynamic-group-plan.json` (planned group creates and rule patches with per-row provenance back to UIAO_152), `dynamic-group-report.json` (post-apply audit trail), and phantom-group findings for the governance findings stream.

**Does NOT:** provision static (assigned) groups, modify membership rules that were not authored from UIAO_152, manage on-premises-synced groups, touch app-role assignments or group-to-group nesting, or auto-delete phantom groups. Apply paths are gated on `dry_run=False`; phantom remediation is human-approved through UIAO_155 Workflow 3.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `AC-2`, `AC-3`, `AC-6`, `CM-8`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **AC-2** Account Management | Primary | Dynamic membership rules express account-group binding declaratively from OrgPath — membership tracks the OrgPath assignment rather than a separate group-management process. |
| **AC-3** Access Enforcement | Primary | Downstream policy systems (Conditional Access, PIM, Intune, Azure Policy) enforce access decisions by referencing these groups as the canonical OrgPath population. |
| **AC-6** Least Privilege | Supporting | OrgPath-bounded membership enables the [`entra-admin-units`](../entra-admin-units/entra-admin-units.html) adapter to scope administrative authority to the appropriate organizational segment. |
| **CM-8** Information System Component Inventory | Supporting | The plan and report artifacts enumerate every OrgTree-* group, its UIAO_152 row, and its membership rule for inventory and audit. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `python-3.12` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `github-hosted` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

Binds to UIAO_152 (Dynamic Group Library) through ADR-036; executable canon at `canon/data/orgpath/dynamic-groups.yaml`, JSON Schema at `schemas/orgpath/dynamic-groups.schema.json`. `plan()` and `apply(dry_run=True)` are offline-safe; `apply(dry_run=False)` issues `POST /groups` and `PATCH /groups/{id}` against Microsoft Graph.

Phantom groups are never auto-applied: per UIAO_152 §Drift they surface as governance findings and route through the Workflow 3 review in UIAO_155.

ZTMM pillar: identity.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- UIAO_007 — OrgTree Modernization: AD to Entra ID
- UIAO_151 — OrgPath Codebook
- UIAO_152 — Dynamic Group Library
- UIAO_155 — Governance Workflow Catalog

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
